entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated brain builds a self-wiring knowledge graph by automatically extracting entities and relationships from agent interactions. It utilizes a hybrid search mechanism that combines vector retrieval with backlink-boosted ranking across 26 modular skills for data ingestion and enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It allows Hermes to evolve its mental models over time and integrates seamlessly with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop to propose and analyze strategies, distilling validated execution data into optimized runtimes. It is designed to improve agent behavior across repeated scenarios, making it ideal for mastering complex, multi-step tasks.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin provides lossless context management using a hierarchical DAG-based engine that ensures no message is ever truly forgotten. It features a three-level escalation logic for history compaction and stores immutable message logs in profile-scoped SQLite databases.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project offers a private, self-hosted backend for cross-session persistence using Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis. It enables Hermes agents to maintain memory across different sessions while supporting any OpenAI-compatible or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory vault that combines vector search, BM25, and graph traversal for hybrid retrieval without cloud dependencies. It specializes in automated decision capture and handoff generation, allowing for seamless state transitions between different local agents.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer that stores persistent data as open-format YAML engrams to eliminate API costs. The system mimics biological memory by implementing automatic decay and strengthening based on how frequently specific information is accessed.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this MCP-native server uses anticipatory caching to prefetch context by monitoring agent session logs. It relies on SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide a low-latency, local-first RAG experience for coding agents.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to operate across 26 security domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io integration standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a comprehensive 109-entry word replacement table. It features a two-pass audit system compatible with Hermes Agent to ensure text remains natural and bypasses automated detection.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It allows Hermes to quickly adopt professional frameworks for marketing and business logic through an npx-based installation process.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open specification specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams, including UML and architecture flows, from natural language descriptions. It supports grid-aligned layouts and specialized tensor shape annotations for machine learning visualizations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Built on the agentskills.io spec, this library allows Hermes to interact with the Chainlink network for cross-chain transfers and data feed monitoring. It provides the necessary tools for smart contract testing and secure oracle operations within an agentic workflow.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave runnable source code and prose documentation into unified .lit.md files. It features an automated tangler that extracts code for execution and syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: This plugin introduces cross-instance shared memory, allowing Hermes to remember past work and extract decision-quality training data. It creates an end-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning replacement models based on the agent's own historical performance.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with built-in support for Stripe and blockchain-based monetization. It automates dependency resolution and local installation, providing a structured marketplace for Hermes capabilities.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official skill set for FLUX image generation, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for Hermes. It supports precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting to ensure high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills for Hermes. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing the agent to programmatically expand its own toolkit through observation.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running missions that provides Hermes with structured memory and milestone-based planning. It uses a local-first state management system to handle handoffs between agent runs via detailed markdown briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks with autonomous tracking and real-time decision logging. It includes built-in rollback procedures and checkpoints to ensure safe recovery during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that utilizes three-layer lazy loading to minimize context window usage for Hermes. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading of configurations without downtime.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This utility converts existing repositories and developer documentation into immutable, version-pinned skills for Hermes. It maps AI instructions directly to source code line numbers, preventing documentation rot in agentic workflows.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing Hermes skills with automated security scanning for prompt injection and data exfiltration. It features a proof-of-use review system and supports micropayments for premium skill developers.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing Hermes to interface with C# based MCP tools. It implements progressive disclosure to save tokens while enabling automatic skill discovery from standard agent directories.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily-updated Nix packages for a wide range of AI coding agents, ensuring reproducible environments for both open-source and proprietary tools. It is the ideal choice for developers who require a stable, version-controlled dependency chain for their Hermes workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing through NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking. It offers pre-configured security policies and a library of over 40 integrated tools, making it the most robust option for sensitive agent operations.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between automated infrastructure and manual customization by supporting both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skill installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It features an integrated workflow engine for scheduled automation and built-in support for local models and GPU monitoring.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This repository offers a streamlined Docker-based sandbox that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and the ability to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless deployments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to handle recurring AI cron jobs and Nous Portal integrations.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed for easy orchestration via Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds and container management.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users who require a private environment to monitor task spend and implement human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support 23 different model providers, including Hermes and local LLMs. It distinguishes itself through persistent agent memory and a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that expands agent capabilities over time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to improve the performance of Hermes Agent on benchmarks. It orchestrates structured candidate mutations to find the most efficient harness code for TBLite and TB2 evaluation tasks.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars in real-time. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics through a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases ranging from research and planning to infrastructure. It ensures high-quality outputs by implementing mandatory quality gates and code reviews at every stage of the multi-agent lifecycle.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It enforces architectural integrity through an eight-phase process where each transition is protected by automated graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project enables ACP-style delegation by routing tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes safety and efficiency through isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It leverages LiteLLM pricing data to provide developers with detailed 3D visualizations and usage analytics for their local and cloud-based workflows.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal experience through custom YAML-based themes and ASCII banners. It features a flexible inheritance system that lets users override specific UI elements like spinners and color schemes while maintaining the core interface functionality.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth. It is particularly valuable for developers requiring native MLOps capabilities, such as vLLM inference management and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, Vessel exposes structured page schemas and durable session states via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It bridges the gap between agents and the web by providing a supervisory sidebar for human auditing and manual intervention during complex workflows.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md introduces a scoped framework for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-specific memories, tools, and instructions within local folders. It enables seamless switching between global settings and specialized agent configurations tailored to the unique requirements of a single workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool identifies structural flaws and ambiguities in agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM API costs. By detecting failure modes like tool loops, it serves as an automated quality gate for integrating Hermes agents into CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes by mapping complex model configurations and personas into the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of workspace files, credentials, and memories to ensure a consistent agent experience across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment stack uses Docker to orchestrate a complete Hermes environment featuring LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local memory. It is optimized for zero-cost operations, providing 29 pre-configured plugins and automated routing for free model inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-framework interface replicates the full Hermes Agent CLI experience through a three-panel layout optimized for both desktop and mobile browsers. It provides a streamlined workspace for simultaneous chat, navigation, and file browsing without the overhead of heavy JavaScript frameworks.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened native web workspace that features real-time SSE streaming for agent outputs and tool call rendering. It offers comprehensive management tools for memory, skills, and background jobs, protected by execution approval prompts and authentication middleware.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway, allowing users to configure and monitor multiple agent profiles from a single panel. It distinguishes itself with detailed token analytics that track costs across different models, platforms, and timeframes.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that establishes direct SSH connections to agents without requiring a middle-layer API or remote daemon. It features a multi-tabbed terminal and specialized tools for managing remote cron jobs and SQLite session stores.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed as a Telegram Mini App, featuring a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It allows users to spawn background agents and monitor live system health metrics like CPU and memory usage directly from their phone.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process by providing guided installation and dependency resolution for the Hermes Agent. It supports a wide array of LLM providers and includes integrated messaging gateways for external platforms like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides real-time activity tracking and cost distribution analysis for Hermes sessions. It features deep conversation inspection via FTS5 search and a read-only interface for viewing configurations and scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that integrates a marketplace for skills, MCP servers, and plugins into a unified interface. It utilizes isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys separate across different agent contexts.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface uses a unique single-file HTML architecture to provide a lightweight, self-hosted assistant UI. It includes a specialized artifact panel for live previews of HTML, SVG, and PDF files generated by the agent.
